seoTitle: Blacksoil vs Blackstone — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Blacksoil and Blackstone. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Blacksoil and Blackstone.
heading: Blacksoil vs Blackstone — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Blacksoil, Queensland and Blackstone, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Blackstone has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Blacksoil (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Blacksoil or Blackstone safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Blacksoil has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Blackstone scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Blacksoil scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Blackstone scores 15/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Blacksoil or Blackstone?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Blackstone has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.07% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
